_comment: Plain-language overrides for tool-native rule output. Lookup key is `<test>:<rule_id>` (where `test` is alfa / axe / pa11y / lint / etc.). Add an entry when the tool's native message is jargony OR when a Drupal-specific fix hint adds value the generic message can't. Otherwise the emitter falls through to the tool's native message + tool-specific impact_category default — most lint rules don't need an entry here.

alfa:SIA-R2.headline: Image without alt text
alfa:SIA-R2.fix_hint: Add an alt attribute that describes the image. Use alt="" only when the image is purely decorative.

alfa:SIA-R1.headline: Page is missing a title
alfa:SIA-R1.fix_hint: Add a non-empty, descriptive <title> element in the page <head> — screen-reader users rely on it to identify the page.

alfa:SIA-R4.headline: Page is missing a valid language
alfa:SIA-R4.fix_hint: Set a valid lang attribute on the <html> element (e.g. lang="en") so assistive tech pronounces content correctly.

alfa:SIA-R11.headline: Link has no accessible name
alfa:SIA-R11.fix_hint: Give the link visible text, or an aria-label / aria-labelledby / title that describes its destination.

alfa:SIA-R12.headline: Button has no accessible name
alfa:SIA-R12.fix_hint: Give the button visible text or an aria-label that describes its action.

alfa:SIA-R14.headline: Visible label is not part of the accessible name
alfa:SIA-R14.fix_hint: Ensure the control's accessible name includes its visible label text — speech-input users say the visible label to activate it.

alfa:SIA-R16.headline: Element is missing a required ARIA state or property
alfa:SIA-R16.fix_hint: Add the ARIA states/properties required by the element's role (per the WAI-ARIA spec).

alfa:SIA-R64.headline: Heading has no accessible name
alfa:SIA-R64.fix_hint: Give the heading non-empty text content (or an accessible name) so it appears in the screen-reader heading list.

alfa:SIA-R87.headline: No skip link to the main content
alfa:SIA-R87.fix_hint: Make the first focusable element a skip link (visible on focus) that targets the main content region.

alfa:SIA-R9.headline: Link text not unique enough to identify the destination
alfa:SIA-R9.fix_hint: Replace generic "Click here" / "Read more" links with text that describes where the link goes.

alfa:SIA-R17.headline: Element hidden from screen readers but still keyboard-reachable
alfa:SIA-R17.fix_hint: If the element is hidden from assistive tech, also remove it from the keyboard tab order (or vice-versa). Pick one.

alfa:SIA-R66.headline: Text contrast lower than WCAG minimum
alfa:SIA-R66.fix_hint: Increase the contrast ratio to at least 4.5:1 for body text or 3:1 for large text (18pt+ or 14pt+ bold).

alfa:SIA-R68.headline: Element has unsupported ARIA role
alfa:SIA-R68.fix_hint: Use a valid ARIA role from the WAI-ARIA spec, or remove the role attribute and rely on native semantics.

alfa:SIA-R111.headline: Click target smaller than 44×44 pixels
alfa:SIA-R111.fix_hint: Make tappable controls at least 44×44 CSS pixels. Touch users miss targets that are too small.

axe:image-alt.headline: Image without alt text
axe:image-alt.fix_hint: Add an alt attribute. Decorative images get alt="" so screen readers skip them.

axe:link-name.headline: Link without accessible text
axe:link-name.fix_hint: Add visible text or an aria-label. Icon-only links need a screen-reader-visible label.

axe:button-name.headline: Button without accessible text
axe:button-name.fix_hint: Add text inside the button or use aria-label. Icon-only buttons need a label assistive tech can read.

axe:color-contrast.headline: Text contrast lower than WCAG minimum
axe:color-contrast.fix_hint: 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text (18pt+ or 14pt+ bold). Check both light and dark themes if the site supports both.

axe:duplicate-id.headline: Duplicate id on the page
axe:duplicate-id.fix_hint: ids must be unique. Repeated ids confuse aria-labelledby, aria-describedby, and label[for] associations.

axe:heading-order.headline: Heading levels skip a step (e.g., h1 → h3)
axe:heading-order.fix_hint: Headings should descend in order. Skipping levels breaks document outline navigation in screen readers.

axe:label.headline: Form control without an associated label
axe:label.fix_hint: Wrap the input in a <label>, or use <label for="id">, or aria-labelledby. Placeholders are NOT labels.

axe:landmark-one-main.headline: Page is missing a <main> landmark
axe:landmark-one-main.fix_hint: Wrap the primary content in <main> so screen-reader users can jump straight to it.

axe:region.headline: Content sits outside any landmark
axe:region.fix_hint: Place all visible content inside a landmark (<main>, <nav>, <aside>, <footer>, etc.) so screen-reader users can navigate by section.

axe:aria-allowed-attr.headline: ARIA attribute not allowed on this role
axe:aria-allowed-attr.fix_hint: Remove the disallowed ARIA attribute or change the element's role to one that supports it. Each ARIA role has a fixed set of allowed attributes (see WAI-ARIA spec).

axe:aria-allowed-role.headline: ARIA role not allowed on this element
axe:aria-allowed-role.fix_hint: Remove the role or change the element. Some HTML elements have implicit roles that conflict with explicit ARIA roles.

axe:aria-command-name.headline: ARIA command without an accessible name
axe:aria-command-name.fix_hint: Add aria-label, aria-labelledby, or visible text to elements with role="button" / "link" / "menuitem" so screen readers can announce them.

axe:aria-hidden-focus.headline: Element hidden from screen readers but still keyboard-focusable
axe:aria-hidden-focus.fix_hint: If the element is hidden via aria-hidden, also remove it from the tab order (tabindex="-1" or remove). Keyboard users should never reach content screen readers can't.

axe:aria-input-field-name.headline: ARIA input field without an accessible name
axe:aria-input-field-name.fix_hint: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby to elements with role="textbox" / "combobox" / "searchbox" / "spinbutton" / "slider".

axe:aria-required-attr.headline: ARIA role missing a required attribute
axe:aria-required-attr.fix_hint: Each ARIA role has required states/properties (e.g., role="checkbox" needs aria-checked). Add the missing attribute or change the role.

axe:aria-required-children.headline: ARIA role missing required child roles
axe:aria-required-children.fix_hint: Container roles (e.g., role="list", "menu", "tablist") require specific child roles. Add the missing children or change the container role.

axe:aria-required-parent.headline: ARIA role missing required parent role
axe:aria-required-parent.fix_hint: Some ARIA roles (e.g., role="listitem", "menuitem", "tab") only make sense inside a specific parent role. Wrap them or change the role.

axe:aria-roles.headline: Invalid ARIA role value
axe:aria-roles.fix_hint: Use a valid role from the WAI-ARIA spec (see https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria/#role_definitions). Typos like role="buton" silently break assistive tech.

axe:aria-toggle-field-name.headline: ARIA toggle field without an accessible name
axe:aria-toggle-field-name.fix_hint: Add aria-label / aria-labelledby / visible text to role="checkbox" / "radio" / "switch" / "menuitemcheckbox" / "menuitemradio".

axe:aria-tooltip-name.headline: ARIA tooltip without an accessible name
axe:aria-tooltip-name.fix_hint: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby to role="tooltip" elements.

axe:aria-valid-attr.headline: ARIA attribute name is not valid
axe:aria-valid-attr.fix_hint: Check spelling — only attributes from the WAI-ARIA spec are recognized (e.g., aria-labelledby, aria-describedby). Typos are silently ignored by assistive tech.

axe:aria-valid-attr-value.headline: ARIA attribute has an invalid value
axe:aria-valid-attr-value.fix_hint: Each ARIA attribute has an enumerated set of valid values (e.g., aria-expanded must be "true" / "false"). Verify against the WAI-ARIA spec.

axe:bypass.headline: Page lacks a way to bypass repeated content
axe:bypass.fix_hint: Add a skip-to-main-content link as the first focusable element, or use landmark roles (<main>, <nav>) so keyboard users can jump past navigation.

axe:document-title.headline: Page is missing a <title>
axe:document-title.fix_hint: Every page needs a <title> in <head> that uniquely describes its content. Drupal usually fills this from the node title — verify the head output.

axe:frame-title.headline: <iframe> missing a title attribute
axe:frame-title.fix_hint: Add title="…" to every <iframe>. Screen readers announce this so users know what the frame contains before entering it.

axe:html-has-lang.headline: <html> element missing lang attribute
axe:html-has-lang.fix_hint: Add lang="en" (or appropriate language code) to <html>. Screen readers use this to switch pronunciation / voice.

axe:html-lang-valid.headline: <html> lang attribute is invalid
axe:html-lang-valid.fix_hint: Use a valid BCP 47 language tag (e.g., "en", "en-US", "es"). Custom strings won't trigger the right screen-reader voice.

axe:image-redundant-alt.headline: Alt text repeats nearby visible text
axe:image-redundant-alt.fix_hint: Screen readers will announce the same content twice. Use alt="" if the image is decorative, or write alt text that adds information the surrounding text doesn't.

axe:input-button-name.headline: <input type="button"> without an accessible name
axe:input-button-name.fix_hint: Set the value attribute (e.g., value="Submit") or add aria-label. Otherwise screen readers announce "button" with no context.

axe:landmark-banner-is-top-level.headline: Banner landmark nested inside another landmark
axe:landmark-banner-is-top-level.fix_hint: Move <header role="banner"> to the top level of the document. Nested banner landmarks confuse landmark navigation in screen readers.

axe:landmark-unique.headline: Duplicate landmark roles need unique labels
axe:landmark-unique.fix_hint: When multiple landmarks share a role (e.g., two <nav>s), give each a distinct aria-label so screen-reader users can tell them apart.

axe:link-in-text-block.headline: Link not visually distinguishable from surrounding text
axe:link-in-text-block.fix_hint: Color alone isn't enough (3:1 contrast against the surrounding text and a non-color cue like underline). Underlines are the safest signal.

axe:list.headline: <ul> or <ol> contains non-<li> children
axe:list.fix_hint: Lists must contain only <li> elements (plus <script> / <template>). Wrap stray content in <li> or use a different element.

axe:listitem.headline: <li> not inside <ul> / <ol> / <menu>
axe:listitem.fix_hint: Wrap the <li> in a <ul>, <ol>, or <menu>. Orphan list items break list-navigation shortcuts in screen readers.

axe:meta-refresh.headline: <meta http-equiv="refresh"> in <head>
axe:meta-refresh.fix_hint: Auto-redirects break the back button and disorient screen-reader users. Use a server-side 301 / 302 redirect or a client-side script that respects user input.

axe:meta-viewport.headline: Meta-viewport blocks user zoom
axe:meta-viewport.fix_hint: Remove user-scalable=no and any maximum-scale<2 from <meta name="viewport">. Recommended baseline: width=device-width, initial-scale=1.

axe:nested-interactive.headline: Interactive element nested inside another
axe:nested-interactive.fix_hint: Avoid putting <button> inside <a> (or vice versa) — keyboard / assistive tech can't reliably target the inner control. Restructure so each interactive control is a sibling.

axe:object-alt.headline: <object> missing alternative text
axe:object-alt.fix_hint: Provide fallback content inside the <object> element or add aria-label. Screen readers can't see embedded objects without an alternative.

axe:role-img-alt.headline: Element with role="img" missing accessible name
axe:role-img-alt.fix_hint: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby to the element. Without it, screen readers announce "image" with no context.

axe:scrollable-region-focusable.headline: Scrollable region not keyboard-focusable
axe:scrollable-region-focusable.fix_hint: Add tabindex="0" to the scrollable container so keyboard users can scroll it. Or add a focusable child element that triggers the scroll.

axe:select-name.headline: <select> without an accessible name
axe:select-name.fix_hint: Wrap the <select> in <label>, use <label for="id">, or add aria-label. Screen readers need a label to describe what the dropdown controls.

axe:skip-link.headline: Skip link not focusable or pointing at a missing target
axe:skip-link.fix_hint: Verify the skip link href matches an id on the page (e.g., href="#main" → <main id="main">) and is focusable (no display:none / visibility:hidden when focused).

axe:svg-img-alt.headline: <svg> with role="img" missing accessible name
axe:svg-img-alt.fix_hint: Add a <title> child element inside the <svg>, or aria-labelledby pointing at one. SVGs without a name are silent to screen readers.

axe:tabindex.headline: Positive tabindex disrupts natural tab order
axe:tabindex.fix_hint: Use only tabindex="0" (focusable, natural order) or tabindex="-1" (programmatically focusable). Positive values create unpredictable jumps for keyboard users.

axe:td-headers-attr.headline: <td headers> references a non-existent header id
axe:td-headers-attr.fix_hint: Verify each id in the headers attribute matches the id of a <th> in the same table. Broken references mean screen readers announce no header for that cell.

axe:th-has-data-cells.headline: <th> has no associated data cells
axe:th-has-data-cells.fix_hint: Either remove the unused <th> or add data cells that reference it. A header that labels nothing is confusing in screen-reader table navigation.

axe:valid-lang.headline: lang attribute on element is invalid
axe:valid-lang.fix_hint: Use a valid BCP 47 language tag. Inline lang attributes (e.g., on a <span>) help screen readers switch pronunciation mid-sentence.

axe:video-caption.headline: <video> missing captions <track>
axe:video-caption.fix_hint: Add a <track kind="captions" srclang="en" src="…"> child for every <video>. Required for deaf and hard-of-hearing users (WCAG 1.2.2).

pa11y:WCAG2AA.Principle1.Guideline1_1.1_.headline: Image without alt text
pa11y:WCAG2AA.Principle1.Guideline1_1.1_.fix_hint: Add an alt attribute that describes the image. Use alt="" only when the image is purely decorative.

pa11y:WCAG2AA.Principle1.Guideline1_4.1_.headline: Text contrast lower than WCAG minimum
pa11y:WCAG2AA.Principle1.Guideline1_4.1_.fix_hint: Increase the contrast ratio to at least 4.5:1 for body text or 3:1 for large text.

pa11y:WCAG2AA.Principle2.Guideline2_4.2_.headline: Link text not unique enough
pa11y:WCAG2AA.Principle2.Guideline2_4.2_.fix_hint: Replace generic link text with text that describes the destination.

lint:phpcs:DrupalPractice.Objects.Global.headline: `\Drupal::` static call inside an injectable context
lint:phpcs:DrupalPractice.Objects.Global.fix_hint: Inject the service via constructor or `create()` instead of calling the global container. Static calls are testing- and DI-hostile.

lint:phpstan:drupal.containerCalls.headline: `\Drupal::` static call inside an injectable class
lint:phpstan:drupal.containerCalls.fix_hint: Inject the service via constructor or `create()` instead of the global container. Static calls are testing- and DI-hostile.

lint:phpstan:drupal.unsafeStatic.headline: Unsafe `new static()` outside a final class
lint:phpstan:drupal.unsafeStatic.fix_hint: Mark the class final, or use `new self()` if subclasses shouldn't override the construction.

lint:phpstan:property.notFound.headline: Access to an undefined property
lint:phpstan:property.notFound.fix_hint: For Drupal entity field access, use `$entity->get('field_name')->value` or cast to a more specific entity interface that declares the field.

lint:phpstan:class.notFound.headline: Reference to an unknown class
lint:phpstan:class.notFound.fix_hint: Check the FQCN — it may have moved (e.g., `Drupal\Core\Path\PathAliasManagerInterface` is now `Drupal\path_alias\AliasManagerInterface`).

lint:phpstan:classConstant.deprecated.headline: Deprecated class constant in use
lint:phpstan:classConstant.deprecated.fix_hint: Replace with the modern equivalent. Drupal core's deprecation message tells you what to switch to (e.g., `FileSystemInterface::EXISTS_REPLACE` → `FileExists::Replace`).

lint:eslint:parsing-error.headline: JavaScript syntax error
lint:eslint:parsing-error.fix_hint: Fix the syntax. ESLint can't lint a file it can't parse — this blocks all other rules from running on it.

lint:cspell:whitelist.headline: Use inclusive language: prefer `allowlist` over `whitelist`
lint:cspell:whitelist.fix_hint: Replace `whitelist` / `whitelisted` with `allowlist` / `allowlisted` (or `allowed` in prose). Same for variable names where reasonable.

lint:cspell:whitelisted.headline: Use inclusive language: prefer `allowlisted` over `whitelisted`
lint:cspell:whitelisted.fix_hint: Replace with `allowlisted`. If the term comes from an external API or library, document the binding in a comment.

lint:cspell:e-mail.headline: Use `email` (no hyphen)
lint:cspell:e-mail.fix_hint: Modern style is `email`. The hyphenated form is dated.

lint:cspell:grey.headline: Use US spelling: `gray`
lint:cspell:grey.fix_hint: Replace `grey` with `gray` to match the project's US-English convention.

lint:cspell:queuing.headline: Use the conventional spelling: `queueing`
lint:cspell:queuing.fix_hint: Replace `queuing` with `queueing` for consistency with Drupal core's spelling.

lint:composer:validate:lock-out-of-sync.headline: `composer.lock` is out of sync with `composer.json`
lint:composer:validate:lock-out-of-sync.fix_hint: Run `composer update --lock` to refresh the lock file without changing dep versions, or `composer update <package>` to bump a specific dep.

lint:composer:audit:abandoned.headline: Abandoned composer dependency
lint:composer:audit:abandoned.fix_hint: The package is no longer maintained. If a replacement is suggested, migrate. Otherwise plan a swap before security advisories accumulate.
